---
title: " "
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    css: styles.css
    title-block-banner: none
    title-block-style: none
    title-block: none
    page-layout: full
    footer:
      left: "© 2026 Javiera Alfaro – Centre for Open Learning, University of Edinburgh"
      right: "Website created with [Quarto](https://quarto.org)"
editor: visual
---

[Pre-University Summer School 2026 · University of Edinburgh]{style="font-size: 0.9rem; color: #777;"}

# 🧪 Exploring Medical Data with R

This is your companion site for the Summer School practical sessions. Over two sessions, you will learn how researchers use data to answer questions about health and medicine.

We will focus on one main idea:

> A research question determines which variables we need, how we summarise the data, and which statistical test we use.

---

## What you will learn

You will learn how to:

- identify categorical and numerical variables;
- import a dataset into R;
- inspect and explore a dataset;
- calculate summary statistics;
- create simple graphs;
- interpret results in the context of a research question;
- understand why hypothesis testing is needed.

---

## The main dataset

Our main example uses a fictional clinical trial dataset called `sbp_demo.csv`.

The trial investigates a new blood pressure medication called **Hypotensol**.

We will ask:

> Do patients receiving the **High Dose Hypotensol** treatment have lower post-treatment systolic blood pressure than patients receiving the **Placebo** treatment?

---

## How the sessions work

### Session 1: Exploring and summarising data

In the first session, we will:

- open RStudio;
- download and use an R Markdown student workbook;
- import the clinical trial dataset;
- identify the variables needed for the research question;
- calculate summary statistics; and
- create visualisations.

### Session 2: Testing research questions

In the second session, we will return to the patterns observed in Session 1 and ask:

> Could this difference have occurred simply by chance?

We will introduce hypothesis testing and learn how statistical tests help researchers make evidence-based conclusions.

---

## Where to start

Start here:

**[Getting Started with RStudio](Getting_started_with_RStudio.html)**

Then continue to:

**[Exploring a Clinical Trial Dataset](Exploring_a_Clinical_Trial_Dataset.html)**
